summary: Djibouti's posture is set in legislation. The law establishing the National Cybersecurity Authority lists post-quantum cryptography in its Article 3 among the strategic emerging technologies the authority is mandated to address for digital sovereignty and national security.
govActors: Autorité Nationale de Cybersécurité (ANC) | National Cybersecurity Authority mandated by law to address post-quantum cryptography among strategic emerging technologies
provenance: ANC creation + PQC in Article 3: Loi n 195/AN/25/9eme L, Journal Officiel de Djibouti, 2025 (ingested T1)
govActor named for the authority the law creates; issuingOrg row is the Republic of Djibouti (Assemblee Nationale / Journal Officiel)
dominantProcess omitted: law names PQC as a mandate area but no standards camp (NIST/ISO/ETSI) - grey beats wrong
No standardFamilies, algorithms, hybrid stance, timeline or target stated

dominantProcess re-classified NIST by best-available signal (low): the law creating the ANC names post-quantum cryptography as a mandate area with no sovereign Djiboutian scheme, so the nascent engagement defaults to the de facto international (NIST) camp. Legislative-mandate signal only, pre-implementation - provisional, flagged for review
